pi_items.source: Web search for POE5 datasets; verified via Principles of Econometrics official website and Lee Adkins' gretl page (learneconometrics.com)
Downloaded POE5 food dataset from http://www.principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/data/csv/food.csv and calculated OLS regression of food_exp on income using Python scipy.stats.linregress
This is a tolerance specification, not a data property
Web search verification of standard influence/leverage diagnostics in regression; confirmed by multiple sources including SFU statistics documentation and Wikipedia on DFFITS
This is a threshold specification, not a data property

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is a real, non-synthetic dataset from Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition (POE5) containing 40 household observations of food expenditure and income. The dataset is available from the official POE5 website and Lee Adkins' gretl data repository. When performing OLS regression of food_exp on income, the slope coefficient is 10.209643, which rounds to the claimed expected value of 10.2. The metadata requirement for keywords 'leverage', 'dffits', and 'hat' aligns with standard influence diagnostic terminology in econometric regression analysis.
